programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: We revisit Julie, who reunited with her birth mother leading to an incredible revelation; and Christina, as she travels 5000 miles to Colombia to spend time with the birth parents, who gave her up in extreme circumstances.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Bushfood Chef, Grass Trees & Organic Wines
programme.desc: Millie Ross meets a chef drawing on his culture to create distinctive dishes, Jerry Coleby-Williams revisits a delightfully individual garden, and Clarence Slockee meets a pair keen to promote their local plants.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Alice surprises comedian Georgie Carroll with a healthy jalapeno falafel dish. Georgie reveals to Alice how she decided to give nursing the chop.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: News Breakfast
programme.desc: James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, overnight developments, interviews and new details on the top stories this morning, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Lillian Rangiah with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's going on this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Kathryn Robinson.

programme.title: Gruen
programme.desc: Australia's favourite multi-award winning, multi-award losing, longest running, and most importantly, the only show about advertising is coming back for an 18th season.

programme.title: Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont-Spelling Bee
programme.desc: An all new line-up of Australian comedians are put to the test by hosts Guy Montgomery and self-effacing oddball Sam Campbell in the series return of Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont-Spelling Bee.

programme.title: Urzila
programme.desc: Urzila ricochets between live stand-up and bombastic sketches, exploring \.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Ros Childs presents the leading news at lunchtime, including the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Bergerac returns for the most challenging case of his career when shocking events unfold at a wedding reception on the Island.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Marlon Williams, Geraldine Hickey, Big Wett & Rhys Nicholson
programme.desc: Marlon Williams, Geraldine Hickey, Big Wett and Rhys Nicholson join Adam, Myf and Alan as they hit such musical heights they have to go into falsetto by the end of it.

programme.title: Anh's Brush With Fame
programme.desc: Anh is joined by one of the founding fathers of emergency medicine: Dr Gordian Fulde. He shares the moments from the frontline of one of Australia's busiest emergency departments that have shaped his world view.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Returning to sixth generational family land, Sam, along with wife Selina, build an off-grid forever home for their three sons from beloved Canberra Red bricks.

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface uncovers a case of life imitating art when a local Amateur Dramatics performer literally dies on stage.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: This time, a former England Rugby player whose lifelong dream has finally become reality; and a mother who found that coming back into the life of child can be a big challenge.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A second visit to Chatsworth House in Derbyshire, where Fiona Bruce and the experts are kept busy as more family treasures are brought from miles around.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.desc: What do the movie Blade Runner, poet Geoffrey Chaucer, Formula 1 driver Daniel Ricciardo and pineapples have in common? They're all topics on Hard Quiz, where four big fans take on the hilarious Tom Gleeson!

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News NSW brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Jeremy Fernandez, plus weather with Tom Saunders.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special, Back Roads guest presenter Rae Johnston tackles the iconic Binns Track through the red heart of Central Australia.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kathryn lived happily on the same street in North London for 60 years. Now 82 and recently widowed, she will build a new, accessible, sustainable home next door.

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: The Assembly's previous graduate students return and are mentored by Leigh Sales as they prepare and interview comedian, book author and renowned radio and TV personality, Andy Lee.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Jade Barker in Perth.

programme.title: The Business
programme.desc: Alicia Barry presents Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing viewers up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, investors, employees, and businesses big and small.

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface uncovers a case of life imitating art when a local Amateur Dramatics performer literally dies on stage.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: This time, a former England Rugby player whose lifelong dream has finally become reality; and a mother who found that coming back into the life of child can be a big challenge.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.desc: It's the start of winter and we have all the practical tips, novel ideas and inspiring stories to get you growing, wherever you are. Costa visits an experimental 'Green House' designed to grow the food its residents need.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Alice and star of comedy and screens, Nina Oyama, throw a Make Your Own Sushi party inspired by Nina's Japanese heritage.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: News Breakfast
programme.desc: James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, overnight developments, interviews and new details on the top stories this morning, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Lillian Rangiah with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's going on this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Kathryn Robinson.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: A descendant of First Nations WWI soldier William Allan Irwin sets out to France to honour his ancestor and return his spirit home to Gomeroi country.

programme.title: Stuff the British Stole
programme.desc: Marc Fennell travels to the Pyramids of Giza and to the very heart of the British Empire to uncover the truth of how the ancient Egyptian mummified remains of a child ended up in Australia's oldest university.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Pitzhanger Manor and Gallery in Ealing, West London, where treasures include a royal paintbox, Italian jewellery and a rare wartime medal awarded to a carrier pigeon.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Ros Childs presents the leading news at lunchtime, including the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: The Assembly's previous graduate students return and are mentored by Leigh Sales as they prepare and interview comedian, book author and renowned radio and TV personality, Andy Lee.

programme.title: Bad Company
programme.desc: It's launch week at The Argyle Theatre. Margie Argyle's uncompromising vision costs her a CEO and lands her with a new one - Julia McNamara, a banker with both eyes on the bottom line and no time for drama.

programme.title: Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont-Spelling Bee
programme.desc: An all new line-up of Australian comedians are put to the test by hosts Guy Montgomery and self-effacing oddball Sam Campbell in the series return of Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont-Spelling Bee.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Sydney couple Anna and Celeste are hoping to transform a rundown Victorian terrace into a modern celebration of colour and character, with not a white wall in sight.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Station No.4 becomes a one-ring circus after Kitty the tiger tamer is murdered, and Murdoch arrests the whole rowdy lot of cunning misfits.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: We catch up with John who returns to the UK to spend time with his siblings; Tracey, who, four years ago was looking for her long-lost brother; and Pauline, who desperately wanted to have her brother at her wedding.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head for Blair Castle, near Pitlochry in Scotland, where the treasures include a chair once used by Einstein, a necklace made from stags' teeth and a rare and valuable watch with no hands.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.desc: Four contestants are after one Big Brass Mug, and host Tom Gleeson is in the way. Expert topics are Scouts Australia, animated film The Incredibles, NBA superstar Michael Jordan, and penny farthing bicycles!

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: On the first Friday of the month, an extended ABC News NSW with special reports and interviews from Stateline. Plus the day's top stories and original reporting. Presented by Lydia Feng, and weather with Tom Saunders.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Autumn: Rare Fruit Collection & Beverly Kills
programme.desc: Costa explores native plants for urban gardens; Josh uncovers a rare fruit collection; Hannah visits a colourful cottage garden; Millie celebrates the humble spud.

programme.title: Patience
programme.desc: A woman is found dead under a poisonous tree. Patience suspects foul play and disagrees with Frankie. The two women fall out as resentments surface.

programme.title: Gruen
programme.desc: Australia's favourite multi-award winning, multi-award losing, longest running, and most importantly, the only show about advertising is coming back for an 18th season.

programme.title: Urzila
programme.desc: Urzila ricochets between live stand-up and bombastic sketches, exploring \.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Craig Smart in Perth.

programme.title: Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont-Spelling Bee
programme.desc: An all new line-up of Australian comedians are put to the test by hosts Guy Montgomery and self-effacing oddball Sam Campbell in the series return of Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont-Spelling Bee.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Station No.4 becomes a one-ring circus after Kitty the tiger tamer is murdered, and Murdoch arrests the whole rowdy lot of cunning misfits.

programme.title: Love Me
programme.desc: As life and love start to flourish for Glen, Clara and Aaron, they're each momentarily jolted back a step to reconsider their choices.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Discover the wildest new music videos on rage. Essential grooves and boundary-pushing visuals from Australia and beyond.
